title: The Broken Washing Machine
summary: A customer calls a repair service because her washing machine is leaking water onto the kitchen floor. The technician asks questions and books a same-day visit.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Hi, I'm calling about my washing machine. It's leaking water all over the kitchen floor.

body.speaker: Technician
body.text: Sorry to hear that. When did the problem start?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: About an hour ago. I started a normal load, and water came out under the door almost immediately.

body.speaker: Technician
body.text: Have you switched the machine off and unplugged it?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Yes. I also turned off the water tap behind the machine.

body.speaker: Technician
body.text: That was the right thing to do. How old is the machine, do you know?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: About six years. It's a Bosch front-loader.

body.speaker: Technician
body.text: Front-loaders often leak from a worn door seal. Can you see anything stuck in the rubber ring?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Let me look. There's a small black piece — looks like part of a button.

body.speaker: Technician
body.text: That's a common cause. A button can cut into the seal during a wash. You'll probably need a new seal.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Can you come today? I have a lot of dirty clothes for the children.

body.speaker: Technician
body.text: I can be there this afternoon between three and five. The call-out fee is forty dollars, and the seal is around sixty.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: That's fine. My address is twenty-two Maple Street, second floor.

body.speaker: Technician
body.text: Got it. Please put some old towels around the machine in case it drips, and don't try to switch it on again. See you this afternoon.

annotations.exampleEn: Our washing machine is very old.
The tap is leaking water again.
I put on a normal load this morning.
Turn off the water tap behind the sink.
The door seal stops water from leaking out.
There's a small rubber ring inside the door.

annotations.meaningVi: phí gọi thợ đến tận nơi
annotations.exampleEn: The call-out fee is paid even if no repair is done.
Water drips from the pipe every minute.
Use old towels to catch the water.

grammarNotes.title: Present continuous for now
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng am/is/are + V-ing để mô tả hành động đang xảy ra ngay lúc này. Trong bài: 'It's leaking water all over the kitchen floor.' (đang rò rỉ ngay lúc cô gọi).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'am/is/are + V-ing' for actions happening right now. From the dialogue: 'It's leaking water all over the kitchen floor' describes what is happening at the moment of the call.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect with 'Have you…?' for completed checks
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng câu hỏi 'Have you + V3?' để hỏi đã hoàn thành một hành động hay chưa. Trong bài: 'Have you switched the machine off and unplugged it?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'Have you + past participle?' to ask if an action has been completed. From the dialogue: 'Have you switched the machine off and unplugged it?'

translationVi:
Customer: Chào anh, tôi gọi về cái máy giặt nhà mình. Nó đang rò nước ra khắp sàn bếp.
Technician: Xin lỗi vì sự cố. Vấn đề bắt đầu khi nào ạ?
Customer: Khoảng một tiếng trước. Tôi bật mẻ giặt bình thường, rồi nước trào ra dưới cửa gần như ngay lập tức.
Technician: Chị đã tắt máy và rút phích chưa?
Customer: Rồi. Tôi cũng khoá vòi nước phía sau máy luôn.
Technician: Đó là việc nên làm. Máy của chị bao nhiêu năm rồi ạ?
Customer: Khoảng sáu năm. Bosch cửa trước.
Technician: Máy cửa trước thường rò do gioăng cửa bị mòn. Chị thử nhìn xem có gì kẹt trong vòng cao su không?
Customer: Để tôi xem. Có một mẩu đen nhỏ — trông như một mảnh nút áo.
Technician: Đó là nguyên nhân phổ biến. Nút áo có thể cứa rách gioăng trong lúc giặt. Có thể chị sẽ cần thay gioăng mới.
Customer: Anh đến hôm nay được không? Tôi có nhiều quần áo bẩn của bọn trẻ.
Technician: Tôi có thể đến chiều nay, khoảng từ ba đến năm giờ. Phí gọi thợ là bốn mươi đô, gioăng thì khoảng sáu mươi.
Customer: Vậy được. Địa chỉ nhà tôi là 22 phố Maple, tầng hai.
Technician: Tôi nhận được rồi. Chị lót vài cái khăn cũ quanh máy phòng khi rỉ nước, và đừng bật máy lại nhé. Hẹn chiều gặp chị.

options: Her washing machine is leaking water
Her washing machine is too loud
Her washing machine is the wrong colour
questions.explanation: 'It's leaking water all over the kitchen floor.'

questions.hint: Listen for a short time period.

questions.prompt: What did she do BEFORE calling?
options: She switched the machine off, unplugged it, and turned off the water tap
She tried to fix it herself
questions.explanation: 'Yes. I also turned off the water tap behind the machine.' (plus switched off and unplugged earlier).
questions.hint: Listen for two or three safety steps she has done.
Listen for a number of years.

questions.prompt: What brand and type is the machine?
questions.hint: Listen for a brand and a type.

questions.prompt: What does the technician suggest is often the cause of leaks in front-loaders?
questions.explanation: 'Front-loaders often leak from a worn door seal.'
questions.hint: Listen for the part of the door.

questions.prompt: What does the customer find stuck in the seal?
options: A small black piece that looks like part of a button
questions.explanation: 'There's a small black piece — looks like part of a button.'
questions.hint: Listen for the colour and what the object looks like.

questions.prompt: Why does the customer want a same-day visit?
options: She is hosting a party tonight
Her children have a lot of dirty clothes
questions.explanation: 'I have a lot of dirty clothes for the children.'
questions.hint: Listen for her reason for urgency.

options: This afternoon between three and five
questions.explanation: 'I can be there this afternoon between three and five.'
questions.hint: Listen for a window of time.

questions.prompt: What is the total expected cost?
options: About one hundred dollars — forty call-out plus sixty for the seal
questions.explanation: 'The call-out fee is forty dollars, and the seal is around sixty.' — together about $100.
questions.hint: Listen for two prices that you need to add.

questions.prompt: What does the technician advise her to do before he arrives?
options: Try to switch the machine on again
Put old towels around the machine and do not switch it on
questions.explanation: 'Please put some old towels around the machine in case it drips, and don't try to switch it on again.'
questions.hint: Listen for two pieces of safety advice.

cloze.template:
Customer: Hi, I'm calling about my washing machine. It's {{b1}} water all over the kitchen floor.
Technician: Sorry to hear that. When did the problem {{b2}}?
Customer: About an hour ago. I started a normal load, and water came out under the door almost immediately.
Technician: Have you switched the machine off and {{b3}} it?
Customer: Yes. I also turned off the water {{b4}} behind the machine.
Technician: That was the right thing to do. How old is the machine, do you know?
Customer: About six years. It's a Bosch {{b5}}-loader.
Technician: Front-loaders often leak from a worn door {{b6}}. Can you see anything stuck in the rubber ring?
Customer: Let me look. There's a small black piece — looks like part of a {{b7}}.
Technician: That's a common cause. A button can cut into the seal during a wash. You'll probably need a new seal.
Customer: Can you come {{b8}}? I have a lot of dirty clothes for the children.
Technician: I can be there this afternoon between three and five. The call-out {{b9}} is forty dollars, and the seal is around sixty.
Customer: That's fine. My address is twenty-two Maple Street, second floor.
Technician: Got it. Please put some old {{b10}} around the machine in case it drips, and don't try to switch it on again. See you this afternoon.

blanks.explanation: 'leaking' — water comes out where it shouldn't.
'unplugged' — removed the plug from the socket.
'front' — a front-loader opens at the front.
'seal' — the rubber that stops water leaking.
'fee' — the cost of the visit itself.
'towels' — to soak up any extra water.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Some people fix small problems at home themselves; others always call a professional. When something breaks at your home, how do you decide whether to try yourself or to phone for help? What might you lose by always doing it yourself?
